summary: Iter-263 frontend-only verification of the iconization sweep across Operator Mgmt + Employee Mgmt page families. All 8 spec items verified end-to-end against the live preview URL using admin@test.com. Every required data-testid is present on the chip strips, IconButtons, and FilterChipSelect filters. No console errors observed across the 6 pages and 2 modals.

geography_page.init-defaults-btn: Not rendered (conditional: countries.length > 0 so hidden — EXPECTED)

access_scopes_page.init-scope-defaults-btn: Not rendered (conditional: scopes.length > 0 so hidden — EXPECTED)

pod_add_member_modal.trigger: Add Member button inside selected pod (Alpha Team)
pod_add_member_modal.member-dept-filter: PRESENT as BUTTON (FilterChipSelect popover trigger) — confirmed NOT a native <select>

pod_assign_operators_modal.trigger: Assign Operators button inside selected pod

critical_code_review_comments: init-defaults-btn (Geography) and init-scope-defaults-btn (EmployeeScope) are only rendered when the respective lists are empty. Current seed data has 6 countries and 3 scopes, so they are intentionally hidden. To exercise these testids in an automated suite in the future, consider seeding an empty-state environment or unconditional render with disabled state when populated.

Pod list cards in PodManagement.jsx (L327) lack a `data-testid="pod-card-{id}"` attribute. Adding one would make automated selection of a specific pod more deterministic than relying on `h3.font-semibold.text-slate-900`.
test_credentials: admin@test.com / testpassword123 (2-step: Login to Your Account -> email -> Continue -> password -> Login)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iconization sweep for Operator + Employee Mgmt families is fully verified. All chip-strip testids and IconButton/FilterChipSelect testids are wired correctly: operators-stats-grid, geography-stats-grid, market-segments-stats-grid, employees-stats-grid, pods-stats-grid, access-scopes-stats-grid; add-country-btn, add-segment-btn, employees-templates-btn, add-employee-btn, add-pod-btn, add-scope-btn; member-dept-filter, operator-status-filter, operator-type-filter, operator-country-filter. The two conditional IconButtons (init-defaults-btn, init-scope-defaults-btn) were not visually exercised because the seeded lists are non-empty; their JSX presence was confirmed via source code review.
